description: Add OpenRouter as a third LLM provider to the Shared AI Layer. OpenRouter uses an OpenAI-compatible API (same SDK, just different baseURL: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1). Includes auto-sync function to fetch models from OpenRouter's API. See tasks/prd-openrouter-provider.md for full PRD.

phases.name: Schema & Keys
phases.description: SQL migration and key validation updates

phases.name: Python Core
phases.description: Python provider branching, streaming, and sync

model_tiers.sonnet: Complex logic stories (provider branching, streaming, refactoring)

userStories.title: SQL migration: Seed OpenRouter models into ai_models registry
userStories.description: Create migration file 007_openrouter_models.sql that INSERTs 8-12 popular OpenRouter models spanning different providers and price tiers. Each row: id (e.g. 'google/gemini-2.0-flash-001'), provider='openrouter', display_name, pricing from OpenRouter's actual rates, context/output token limits, supports_streaming, supports_tools, is_default=false, is_active=true. Do NOT modify existing model rows.
acceptance_criteria: Migration file exists at supabase/migrations/007_openrouter_models.sql
Seeds 8-12 models spanning budget (gemini-flash, deepseek), mid-tier (gemini-pro, mistral-large), and premium (llama-405b, qwen-72b) tiers

Each model row includes: id, provider='openrouter', display_name, cost_per_input_token, cost_per_output_token, max_context_tokens, max_output_tokens, supports_streaming, supports_tools, is_default=false, is_active=true
Pricing reflects OpenRouter's actual rates (check https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models for current pricing)
No existing model rows from previous migrations are modified
Migration SQL is syntactically valid
userStories.notes: Reference existing seed data format in 002_ai_layer_usage_and_models.sql. OpenRouter pricing is per-token (not per-million), so values will be very small decimals. The ai_models.provider column is TEXT (unconstrained), so 'openrouter' works without schema changes.

userStories.title: TypeScript: Add OpenRouter key validation and update .env.example
userStories.description: Update validateKey() in packages/ai-core/src/keys.ts to support 'openai' and 'openrouter' providers (currently only supports 'anthropic'). For OpenAI validation, make a minimal API call using the openai package. For OpenRouter validation, use the OpenAI SDK with baseURL='https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'. Also add OPENROUTER_API_KEY to .env.example. Note: resolveKey() already works generically (uses provider.toUpperCase()+'_API_KEY' pattern) — no changes needed there.
acceptance_criteria: validateKey() supports provider 'anthropic' (existing, no regression)
validateKey() supports provider 'openai' using OpenAI SDK minimal API call
validateKey() supports provider 'openrouter' using OpenAI SDK with baseURL='https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
InvalidKeyError thrown for 401 responses from any provider
Generic error thrown for network/other failures
.env.example includes OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-your-key
TypeScript compiles with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: resolveKey() already maps provider to env var via process.env[`${provider.toUpperCase()}_API_KEY`], so OPENROUTER_API_KEY is auto-resolved. Only validateKey() needs changes.

userStories.title: TypeScript: Add OpenRouter provider to chat() method
userStories.description: Update chat() in packages/ai-core/src/client.ts to handle provider === 'openrouter'. The OpenRouter branch should create an OpenAI client with baseURL: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1' and add HTTP-Referer and X-Title headers per OpenRouter best practices. Since OpenRouter uses OpenAI-compatible API format, extract shared helper functions for message formatting and tool transformation that both 'openai' and 'openrouter' branches can reuse (DRY). Update the provider type to include 'openrouter'. Token counting, cost calculation, usage logging, credit checks, and error mapping must all work correctly for the new provider.
acceptance_criteria: chat() handles provider === 'openrouter' from model registry
OpenRouter branch creates OpenAI client with baseURL: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
HTTP-Referer and X-Title headers added for OpenRouter requests via defaultHeaders
Shared helper function(s) extracted for OpenAI message/tool formatting (used by both openai and openrouter branches)
resolveKey() called with provider from model registry (not hardcoded)
Usage logging records provider: 'openrouter' correctly
Credit pre-check and post-deduction work for managed keys
Error mapping (401 -> AUTHENTICATION_ERROR, 429 -> RATE_LIMIT, 5xx -> PROVIDER_ERROR) works for OpenRouter
Provider type updated to include 'openrouter' in the type assertion
Existing Anthropic and OpenAI chat tests still pass
TypeScript compiles with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Key pattern: new OpenAI({ apiKey: key, baseURL: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1', defaultHeaders: { 'HTTP-Referer': 'https://shared-ai-layer.app', 'X-Title': 'Shared AI Layer' } }). The OpenAI branch at lines 206-248 of client.ts can be refactored to share logic with OpenRouter.

userStories.title: TypeScript: Add OpenAI/OpenRouter streaming to chatStream()
userStories.description: Update chatStream() in packages/ai-core/src/client.ts to support OpenAI and OpenRouter providers (currently throws 'Streaming only supported for Anthropic' at line 343). Use client.chat.completions.create({ stream: true }) for OpenAI-format streaming. Map OpenAI stream chunks to existing ChatChunk interface: emit start_stream, text_delta (from chunk.choices[0].delta.content), and stop_stream. Capture final token counts from stream usage. OpenRouter uses identical streaming format.
acceptance_criteria: chatStream() no longer throws for non-Anthropic providers
OpenAI streaming uses client.chat.completions.create({ stream: true, stream_options: { include_usage: true } })
OpenRouter streaming uses same OpenAI streaming logic with custom baseURL
start_stream chunk emitted at stream start
text_delta chunks emitted for each content delta from choices[0].delta.content
stop_stream chunk emitted with final token counts (tokensIn, tokensOut)
Token counts captured from stream usage data
Usage logged on stream completion (fire-and-forget)
Error handling maps OpenAI/OpenRouter errors to existing error types
Existing Anthropic streaming tests still pass
TypeScript compiles with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: OpenAI streaming format: for await (const chunk of stream) { chunk.choices[0]?.delta?.content }. Usage comes from the final chunk when stream_options.include_usage is set. Shared helper for creating OpenAI/OpenRouter clients should be reused from S03.

userStories.title: TypeScript: OpenRouter model auto-sync function
userStories.description: Create a syncOpenRouterModels() function that fetches all available models from OpenRouter's public API (GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models, no auth required) and upserts them into the ai_models table. Map OpenRouter response fields: id -> model id, pricing.prompt -> cost_per_input_token, pricing.completion -> cost_per_output_token, context_length -> max_context_tokens, top_provider.max_completion_tokens -> max_output_tokens, name -> display_name, provider is always 'openrouter'. New models get inserted, existing models get updated (pricing, context_length), models removed from OpenRouter API get is_active=false (soft delete). Skip models with missing or zero pricing. Return summary { inserted, updated, deactivated }.
acceptance_criteria: New file packages/ai-core/src/sync.ts exists with syncOpenRouterModels() exported
Fetches GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models (no auth header needed)

Maps OpenRouter fields correctly: id, name->display_name, pricing.prompt->cost_per_input_token, pricing.completion->cost_per_output_token, context_length->max_context_tokens, top_provider.max_completion_tokens->max_output_tokens
Provider is always set to 'openrouter' for synced models
New models are inserted with is_active=true
Existing models are updated with latest pricing and context lengths
Models no longer in OpenRouter API response get is_active=false
Models with missing pricing or pricing.prompt='0' are handled (either skipped or inserted with zero cost)
Function returns { inserted: number, updated: number, deactivated: number }
Handles API errors gracefully (network failure, rate limit) without crashing
Uses safeParseFloat pattern from models.ts for parsing pricing strings
sync.ts exported from packages/ai-core/src/index.ts
TypeScript compiles with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: OpenRouter pricing format is string per-token e.g. '0.000003'. Use the existing safeParseFloat() from models.ts. The /api/v1/models endpoint returns { data: [{ id, name, pricing: { prompt, completion }, context_length, top_provider: { max_completion_tokens } }] }. Use Supabase upsert with onConflict: 'id' for the insert/update logic.

userStories.title: TypeScript: Tests for OpenRouter provider
userStories.description: Create comprehensive tests for all new OpenRouter functionality. Test chat() with OpenRouter provider (mock API responses), verify OpenAI client created with correct baseURL, test streaming with OpenRouter, test key validation, test model sync function with mocked API response, test error mapping. All tests use vitest with mocked dependencies (no real API calls).
acceptance_criteria: New test file packages/ai-core/src/__tests__/openrouter-provider.test.ts exists
Tests for chat() with OpenRouter provider verify correct baseURL and headers
Tests for chat() verify shared helper functions work for both openai and openrouter
Tests for streaming with OpenRouter provider
Tests for key validation with 'openrouter' provider
Tests for syncOpenRouterModels() with mocked /api/v1/models response
Tests for sync: insert new models, update existing, deactivate removed
Tests for error mapping (401, 429, 5xx from OpenRouter)
Tests verify usage logging records 'openrouter' as provider
Tests verify BYOK key resolution works for 'openrouter' provider
All new tests pass with npx vitest run
All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
TypeScript compiles with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Follow existing test patterns from client.test.ts and keys.test.ts. Mock the OpenAI constructor, the fetch/httpx calls for sync, and Supabase client. Use vi.mock() for dependency mocking.

userStories.title: Python: Add provider branching for OpenAI and OpenRouter to chat()
userStories.description: Update backend/ai_core/client.py to detect provider from model registry (currently hardcoded to 'anthropic') and branch chat() logic for all three providers. Add 'openai' Python package import. Anthropic branch must be preserved exactly as-is (no regressions). OpenAI branch creates OpenAI(api_key=key) client with standard message/tool formatting. OpenRouter branch creates OpenAI(api_key=key, base_url='https://openrouter.ai/api/v1') client. Update resolve_key() call to use provider from model registry instead of hardcoded 'anthropic'. Update all usage logging to record correct provider.
acceptance_criteria: openai Python package imported (from openai import OpenAI)
chat() detects provider from model registry via get_model() return value
Anthropic branch preserved exactly as-is (no regressions)
OpenAI branch creates OpenAI(api_key=key) client
OpenRouter branch creates OpenAI(api_key=key, base_url='https://openrouter.ai/api/v1') client
Message formatting converts to OpenAI format for openai/openrouter: [{'role': 'user', 'content': '...'}]
Tool/function calling format works for OpenAI/OpenRouter (tools parameter)
Token counting extracts from OpenAI response: response.choices[0].message.content, response.usage.prompt_tokens/completion_tokens
Cost calculation uses existing calculate_cost() function
Usage logging records correct provider string (not hardcoded 'anthropic')
resolve_key() called with provider from model registry (not hardcoded 'anthropic')
OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var used as managed fallback for openrouter provider
Error mapping works for OpenAI/OpenRouter status codes
openai package added to requirements.txt or pyproject.toml
All existing Python tests pass (no regressions)
userStories.notes: The model object returned by get_model() has a .provider attribute. Use it: provider = model.provider. Key pattern for OpenRouter: OpenAI(api_key=key, base_url='https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'). The Python openai package uses response.choices[0].message.content for text and response.usage for tokens.

userStories.title: Python: Add streaming for OpenAI/OpenRouter providers
userStories.description: Update chat_stream() in backend/ai_core/client.py to support OpenAI and OpenRouter providers (currently hardcoded Anthropic-only with os.getenv('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY')). Use openai package streaming: client.chat.completions.create(stream=True). Map OpenAI stream chunks to existing ChatChunk/ChatChunkDelta types. OpenRouter uses same logic with custom base_url. Update key resolution to use provider from model registry.
acceptance_criteria: chat_stream() detects provider from model registry
Anthropic streaming preserved exactly as-is (no regressions)
OpenAI streaming uses client.chat.completions.create(stream=True, stream_options={'include_usage': True})
OpenRouter streaming uses same OpenAI streaming logic with custom base_url
start_stream, text_delta, stop_stream chunks emitted correctly for OpenAI/OpenRouter
text_delta extracted from chunk.choices[0].delta.content
Final token counts captured from stream usage data
Usage logged on stream completion with correct provider
Key resolution uses resolve_key() with provider from model registry (not hardcoded)
OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var used as managed fallback for openrouter
All existing Python tests pass (no regressions)
userStories.notes: Python OpenAI streaming pattern: for chunk in stream: if chunk.choices[0].delta.content: yield text_delta. Usage from final chunk when stream_options include_usage is set. The chat_stream() method currently hardcodes ANTHROPIC_API_KEY at line 174 — this needs to use resolve_key() with proper provider.

userStories.title: Python: OpenRouter model auto-sync function
userStories.description: Create sync_openrouter_models() function in backend/ai_core/sync.py that mirrors the TypeScript sync function. Fetches GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models, maps fields to ai_models table columns, upserts via Supabase client. Uses httpx or requests for the HTTP call. Returns summary dict { inserted, updated, deactivated }.
acceptance_criteria: New file backend/ai_core/sync.py exists with sync_openrouter_models() function
Fetches GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models (no auth needed)
Maps OpenRouter fields to ai_models columns identically to TypeScript version
Upserts: new models inserted, existing updated, removed models set is_active=false
Returns dict: {'inserted': N, 'updated': N, 'deactivated': N}
Uses httpx or requests (add to requirements.txt/pyproject.toml if not present)
Handles API errors gracefully without crashing
sync.py importable from backend.ai_core package
userStories.notes: Mirror the TypeScript sync logic from S05. Use the same field mapping. The Python supabase client has the same upsert API pattern. Add httpx to dependencies if using it.

userStories.title: Documentation: Update README and config for OpenRouter
userStories.description: Update README.md to list OpenRouter as the third provider alongside Anthropic and OpenAI. Add usage examples for OpenRouter (chat, streaming, BYOK). Document the auto-sync function with usage instructions. Update architecture diagram to show 3 providers. Ensure .env.example includes OPENROUTER_API_KEY (should already be done in S02).
acceptance_criteria: README.md lists OpenRouter as third provider
Architecture/provider diagram updated to show 3 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter)
Usage examples for OpenRouter chat, streaming, and BYOK added to README
Auto-sync function documented with usage instructions
Model registry section mentions auto-sync capability
.env.example includes OPENROUTER_API_KEY
No broken links or formatting issues in README
userStories.notes: Keep documentation concise. Focus on what's different about OpenRouter (baseURL trick, 100+ models, auto-sync). The .env.example update should already be done in S02 but verify it here.
